title: Terrible Toxic Work Culture
pros: Paid time off and flexibility for full time workers. Otherwise I will give 0 stars if possible. Most people full time workers in teams I know will just simply stay at home and not show up. One employee got 6 months maternity leave and after she returned she would simply refuse to show up or do anything upon return to work for months. When she was held accountable she just put the blame on a contractor so that she can keep her job. This is just an example of how being a full time employee in JnJ spring house allows you the flexibility to keep your job and pay without literally doing anything. Full time employee can play blame game on contractors as their full time job and thatâs what they are getting a high pay for.
cons: * They hire contractors to do basically everything. * Full time workers just sit at home and relax. Just pray that you should end up in a team that takes accountability. * Full time workers (team specific) just sit at home and relax thanks to their flexible schedule. * They do not give proper accommodations like monitors or desks or anything if you request one due to health condition. * Lack of empathy to the core. Sad to work in a place full of people who donât have any values at all. * If you work as a contractor in this Spring House JnJ I empathize for what youâre going through.

title: They donât live their Credo
pros: Global company. Multi City company
cons: J&J has in its CREDO that one of its responsibilities is with the employees. It says âThey must have a sense of security, fulfillment and purpose in their jobs... We must support the health and well-being of our employees and help them fulfill their family and other personal responsibilities. Nothing farther from the true!! Itâs a lie. After 15 years with J&J and been a single mother I was impacted by a ârestructuringâ and lost my job. After having Exceed in more than one consecutive year on the performance review I cannot even get thru screening on roles at the same level as I was (manager) even though I have related experiences. It is hurtful that the company you loved and dedicated years of life see you as just a number and get rid of you without caringâ¦not even a little. Unbelievable. I feel betrayed and fooled.

title: Company was amazing till we had Joaquin
body: Company was amazing till we had Joaquin
pros: It was a great company, with amazing collaborative culture, slow growth but benefits and work life balance, from tuition reimbursements, gym to childcare facilities, if you go above and beyond you might not be promoted but were given stocks even at managerial level. There were preferences for internal candidates, folks felt like home until Covid hit and Joaquin became the new CEO
cons: Today this is any other company after almost a decade, feel sad that I called it home, if you are a women or a women of color this isnât the place anymore, credo discussions and patient focus is just a facade itâs all about numbers and wall street, folks who are lifers are also treated like nobody, Folks who are good at managing up are the ones you can make the cut, some folks who hide and do nothing keep on staying and the high performers are sidelined or let go. HR partners are of no use, they want people to leave as attrition is still low! They want to hire externally, more European leaders at the top?

title: Expanding Patient Assistance
pros: I love working for J&J for multiple reasons. I enjoy that J&J has adapted as a large company to the demands of a modern workforce while keeping some of the tried and true cultural elements of a mature company. For example, incredibly generous time off, instead of "unlimited PTO". Territories are not unusually large for field teams and they are growing their patient support options through acquisitions of patient support hubs. The different teams work hard to make sure folks in different functions are supported. Such as national contracts are in place quickly and we feel the results downstream on a local level, or we can reach out to people in different functions to have candid conversations about what isn't happening that we've been told to expect. There's a great amount of partnership that's possible if you're willing to reach out. That part of the company culture is great to be a part of.
cons: While the company has made huge strides by purchasing the PSC (Patient Service Center) and integrating the company's personnel as J&J employees, they are a sub par hub with training lapses. Many of the interactions I have with them lack accountability and professionalism. I think this hub would benefit from a different model and increased incentivization to perform at a higher level for patients. I worry what patients experience when relying on the PSC for information regarding their treatments and I often do not feel our hub counterparts are genuinely our partners who understand the responsibility they have to produce results for patients.

cons: J&J is big and complicated. If you think you know how to work in a complicated environment, they take it up a couple of levels. title: Toxic work environment
pros: Remote work Healthcare benefits are good
cons: The micromanaging in this department is ridiculous. You can be meeting all metrics and they will still treat you as if you failed to meet expectations. Arizona has a law called "Arizona Sick Time". Johnson & Johnson provides you the hours but treats them exactly like pto. If you are sick today and need to use sick time, they will mark you down as unexcused. You basically end up reprimanded for using your sick time. The way they do pto requests is ridiculous. Your requests are usually denied. They open request by quarter. If you attempt to apply for a different position, your manager will expect you to inform them before applying. Overall Johnson & Johnson ruins your work like balance.

cons: There is no loyalty with the company as they no longer stand by the Credo they so commit to. It is all a numbers game with the company. They focus on and how long it takes to complete a call. If taking too long you are called out for not using the correct allotted time for a call. What happened to taking care of the customer no matter how long it takes?

title: Good Company, Common Challenges
pros: Iâve never seen a company that tries as hard to keep employment impacted employees within the company by helping to find them new roles. Iâve the last 5 years Iâve seen 5 rounds of layoffs. Each time most of the impacted employees were able to find new positions before their last day of work. Also, the employee pension plan is a great thing. If that wasnât in place I think a lot of people would look for other jobs more quickly, but the long term value creates a significant hit if a person chooses to leave.
cons: The company is way behind technically. Old systems that donât talk to each other, lots of manual work requiring headcount that would not be needed if technology adoption were faster, and development and launch of products without fully developed remote diagnostic ability puts it 15 years+ behind competitors. The employee loyalty mentioned above also means that a lot of longer term employees get promoted to senior roles, and not many senior roles are hired from outside. This leads to a fair number of senior people leading business groups for technology they know nothing about. This creates gaps and lots of need for employees to find bandaid ways to cover and keep things moving.

title: J&J's Dark Side: What They Don't Want You to Know
body: J&J's Dark Side: What They Don't Want You to Know
pros: If you are looking for a job, this is one.
cons: Base salaries here are below market average, and career advancement appears to be heavily influenced by management's personal opinions, rather than demonstrable skills. Johnson & Johnson values unwavering loyalty, even at the expense of skill development. The work environment is quite toxic, with a strong emphasis on internal competition. Employees are expected to consistently praise management decisions, and any form of criticism is generally unwelcome. The company's health insurance benefits are inadequate. Be prepared for a total compensation package that falls short of initial expectations. J&J is mandating a return to the office, which forces some employees to commute significant distances. Technology and infrastructure are lagging, resulting in frequent, minor technical issues. There's also a noticeable pattern of shifting roles and promotions to resources in Asia. I would advise against pursuing employment with this company.

title: A Company in Transition
pros: Legacy of Excellence - Having been with the company for many years, I can attest to its once vibrant and inspiring culture. It was a place where employees felt valued and where the mission to positively impact humanity was more than just the Credo on the wall. Strong Compensation and Benefits - While the culture has shifted, the company still offers competitive pay.
cons: Cultural Decline - The divestiture of the consumer division marked a turning point, leading to a shift away from core values. What was once a mission-driven organization is now singularly focused on financial outcomes, often at the expense of employee morale and purpose. Identity Crisis - The company is struggling to redefine itself post-divestiture. This lack of a clear direction has resulted in conflicting priorities and a sense of instability across teams. Eroding Trust - Transparency and open communication, once hallmarks of the culture, have diminished. Employees are now left in the dark about decisions that significantly impact their roles and futures. Loss of Vision - The cultural decline has alienated employees who were drawn to J&J for its Credo, which now seems to be just decorations on the wall.

title: Strong brand and benefits but....
pros: Significant brand reputation and good long-term benefits. Still offers a pension but has changed unless you were grandfathered in.
cons: Situations and people are not what they seem from initial interactions. Over time, troubling trends were observed in senior management decisions. The appointment of individuals to leadership roles who lacked the necessary qualifications and expertise led to inefficiencies and decisions that did not always make sense and lowered morale. Certain senior managers promoted toxic practices (eg lack of mutual respect, favoritism, lack of transparency, insufficient support impacting work-life balance). Despite frequent restructuring, these underqualified and dysfunctional leaders remain in their roles (or get promoted). While diversity and inclusion was externally promoted at one time, there is very little diversity amongst the many layers of management. Limited opportunities for advancement for people who do not "look" or speak a certain way despite qualifications and dedication,

title: Strike the right balance to attract and maintain key talent
body: Strike the right balance to attract and maintain key talent
pros: Excellent benefits, great salary, prestigious brand name and excellent cross functional growth opportunities. You can truly start at an entry level role and move across disciplines for career growth which is phenomenal.
cons: Losing experienced talent because of three day in office mandate, badge swipe reports, and open seating at Technology Services. The Neighborhood Manager walks the floors telling employees to remove plants, photos, personal items from desks. Quite often employees canât find a place to sit because consultants and contractors take up all the seats. It feels more like a prison than an upbeat, collaborative work environment.

cons: â¢ The entire in-house design department was dissolved, showcasing leadership's lack of understanding of the department and its value. â¢ I was brought on for projects only to have them pulled out from under me and farmed out to agencies (who didn't understand the pharma industry and therefore made lots of mistakes). â¢ When I converted from a contractor to employee, my pay equated to the same as I made hourly, which was far below competitive, let alone for a New York City-based office.

title: Johnson & Johnson: A Rewarding Career with Room for Improvement
body: Johnson & Johnson: A Rewarding Career with Room for Improvement
pros: Positive Work Environment: I experienced a collaborative culture that fosters personal and professional growth and employees have access to various training programs and opportunities for advancement within the organization. J&J provides competitive benefits packages, including health insurance, retirement plans, and wellness programs.
cons: Complex Organizational Structure: the company's large size and complex structure can lead to bureaucratic processes and slower decision-making. Management Variability: Experiences with management can vary; challenges in communication and leadership styles are not uncommon. Workload Demands: Certain roles may involve high workloads and pressure to meet targets, which can impact work-life balance.

title: Favoritism
pros: Great benefits and a lot of paid time off.
cons: Favoritism is what brings you to the top of the company at this certain site. You can be late, your lead can be late and leave early, your supervisor can barely be found, yet you are the only one that will get sacked. Everyone else OUTSIDE of that site appreciates you and your work but once a certain someone doesn't like you, it's over. No need to overperform, it doesn't help your case.

title: Cult like culture with little room for change
body: Cult like culture with little room for change
pros: Better parental leave policy than most pharma companies. Pay upon hiring is good.
cons: Promotions & competition for roles dependent on time in J&J & who you know vs actual ability or potential to do job. Everyone touts years within J&J as a measurement of experience at beginning of every intro to isolate out new employees. What youâve done outside doesnât count towards your qualifications. Connected & networked employees spend 18 months in a role just enough to make superficial accomplishments then leave for next position/promotions. Meanwhile employees focused on actually doing the work or those who recently joined thinking theyâll have career advancement will struggle to get next position. Itâs not fair hiring practices and you donât stand a chance if you just apply to open roles. Donât come thinking youâll be able to move up after joining. Your merits donât matter here itâs who you know and as a newbie you wonât know as many people.

title: A strong culture of favoritism and retaliation
body: A strong culture of favoritism and retaliation
pros: - Generous employee benefits, especially if you make it to the 20-year mark - Company match to charities is the best in the market
cons: - Very little protection and recourse for employees who have bad managers - Complaints sent to employee relations don't lead to any action, and managers can retaliate with no repercussions - The culture supports favoritism and less merit-based - Jobs are oversold during the recruitment process, and often lead to overqualified people doing work that is not aligned to what they were told

title: RN
pros: I am an RN who is a team player and always willing to go above and beyond to ensure the well-being of my patients. My strong attention to detail and excellent communication skills allow me to provide top-notch care to those in need. I am passionate about making a positive impact in the lives of others and am dedicated to continuously learning and growing in my field.
cons: One con of being a registered nurse is that I am a perfectionist. I need to realize that perfection is not always attainable in a fast-paced and constantly changing healthcare environment. It can be challenging to accept that mistakes may happen despite my best efforts. Learning to prioritize and delegate tasks effectively while still maintaining high standards of care is a skill that I am continuously working on developing.

cons: Constant reorganizations, extremely poor handling of layoffs. Reorganizations in big pharma are par for the course, but I have to say that I was extremely disappointed in senior leadership in how they handled things. It is not okay to lie to your employees and senior leadership did not reach out to any of the affected employees to see if we could use any help to find a new position. It seems they were only focused on negotiating their own severance agreement and couldn't care less whether us worker bees would be alright.

title: The company is too large and overburdened with management and red tape.
body: The company is too large and overburdened with management and red tape.
pros: The offered good pay and benefits as most any large company. It was rewarding working for a company whose goal was to improve the lives of people. This was the most diverse workforce that I have worked with in my career.
cons: They are very large and overburdened with red tape, too many procedures to manage a single process, a lot of individuals that base their approvals on their own opinions instead of the requirements in the procedures. There was no HR on site, instead they direct you to access or call a remote group that was not very helpful. The company relies to much on contract workers so that it is hard to recruit and retain good employees. Upper management is focused on acquiring new companies and does a poor job of integrating and developing them.

title: FST
pros: Being a Field Service Technician has several positive aspects. The work is diverse and challenging, allowing for continuous improvement of technical skills. Additionally, it offers the satisfaction of solving customer problems, along with flexible hours and the opportunity to work in different locations. It also provides the possibility to travel and see new places, and there is a consistently high demand for technicians in many industries.
cons: However, it also has its disadvantages. Frequent travel can be exhausting and affect personal life. The work can be done in difficult or uncomfortable environments and is physically demanding. It also requires working unconventional hours, including nights and weekends. Solving technical problems under pressure can be stressful, and it is necessary to stay updated with new technologies through continuous training.
